session.assistant:
Perfect! I've read all 10 files. Here's my report:
## Summary
**9 out of 10 files** contain references to "Printing" or "printing":
### Files with "Printing" References:
1. **analysis_ops/__init__.py** - Line 10: `- printing: PrintingGitAnalysisOps` 2. **branch_ops/__init__.py** - Line 12: `- printing: PrintingGitBranchOps` 3. **commit_ops/__init__.py** - Line 11: `- printing: PrintingGitCommitOps` 4. **config_ops/__init__.py** - Line 10: `- printing: PrintingGitConfigOps` 5. **rebase_ops/__init__.py** - Line 11: `- printing: PrintingGitRebaseOps` 6. **remote_ops/__init__.py** - Line 11: `- printing: PrintingGitRemoteOps` 7. **repo_ops/__init__.py** - Line 10: `- printing: PrintingGitRepoOps` 8. **status_ops/__init__.py** - Line 11: `- printing: PrintingGitStatusOps` 9. **tag_ops/__init__.py** - Line 11: `- printing: PrintingGitTagOps`
### File WITHOUT "Printing" References:
10. **worktree/__init__.py** - Contains only: `"""Git worktree operations subgateway."""` (No printing references)
### Pattern Observed:
All 9 gateway submodules (except worktree) follow an identical documentation pattern listing four implementation variants: - `abc` (abstract interface) - `real` (production implementation) - `fake` (test fake) - `dry_run` (dry-run variant) - `printing` (printing/logging variant)
The `worktree/__init__.py` file is much simpler and lacks this standard documentation structure.
